text: The most exhausting thing you can be is fake.
author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

text: Politics is not the art of the possible. It consists in choosing between the disastrous and the unpalatable.
author: John Kenneth Galbraith

text: The historian's task is not to disturb the sleep of those who live in error.
author: E. H. Carr

text: If you would understand anything, observe its beginning and its development.
author: Aristotle

text: The market has no morality. The market has prices.
author: Adam Tooze

text: Never give your real name to a survey.
author: advice from a community organiser in Bologna, anonymous on request

text: A society grows great when old men plant trees in whose shade they shall never sit.
author: Greek proverb

text: The first job of an organiser is to keep showing up.
author: paraphrasing Marshall Ganz

text: We do not work off emails. We work off official documents and government positions.
author: Pedro Sánchez, April 2026

text: Brussels does what Brussels can. Member states do what they want. The space in between is the only place left to organise.
author: the conclusion of every workshop I run

text: All that is solid melts into air, all that is holy is profaned.
author: Marx, paraphrased every twenty years since

text: If you can't explain it to a friend over a coffee, you don't understand it yet.
author: rule one for policy writing
